question: My oga wan pay me with provisions and soap instead of money for this month. E fit do am?
expected_answer: No — wages must be paid in legal tender (money), not goods or vouchers. Any contract wey try pay with something else na illegal and e null and void [Labour Act s.1(1)].

question: I don work for my company for 7 years now. How much notice dem suppose give me before dem sack me?
expected_answer: One month's written notice (5 years and above band, s.11(2)(d)); or dem fit pay you instead of notice [s.11(6)].

question: My boss say I come late so e go deduct N5,000 from my salary. E fit do that one?
expected_answer: No — deductions for fines are prohibited except where the law expressly permits am [s.5(1)]. And total deductions for one month no fit pass one-third of your wages [s.5(7)].

question: I just start this job 3 months ago and dem say dem wan fire me. How much notice dem suppose give me?
expected_answer: One day's notice (3 months or less band, s.11(2)(a)); or dem fit pay you for that one day instead [s.11(6)].

question: My employer dey pay us salary every 3 months instead of every month. E be like say e no follow law. Which law e dey break?
expected_answer: Wages must be paid at intervals of not more than one month [Labour Act s.15]. Paying every 3 months dey against this provision.

question: Dem sack me from work without any notice. I don work there for 4 years. Wetin I fit do?
expected_answer: You entitled to two weeks' notice (2-5 years band, s.11(2)(c)) or payment in lieu of notice [s.11(6)]. You fit report to the nearest labour office if dem refuse to pay.

question: The company where I dey work say dem dey do redundancy. Na me last to enter the department. Dem fit select me first to go?
expected_answer: Yes — for redundancy, the principle of 'last in, first out' (LIFO) must apply for each category of workers [Labour Act s.20(1)(b)]. The employer must also inform the union or workers' representative about the reasons and extent of the redundancy [s.20(1)(a)].

question: My employer no give me any employment letter since I start work 2 years ago. E suppose give me contract?
expected_answer: Yes — not later than three months after starting, the employer must give you a written statement specifying the terms of employment [Labour Act s.7(1)]. The statement must include the name of the employer, your name, nature of employment, date of first appointment, wage rate and interval, hours of work, holidays, and notice period [s.7(2)].

question: My landlord for Lagos say I must pay am 2 years rent upfront before e go give me the flat. E fit do that one?
expected_answer: No — e unlawful for landlord to demand or receive more than one year's rent from a new tenant [Lagos Tenancy Law s.4(3)]. E be offence wey fit get N100,000 fine or 3 months imprisonment [s.4(5)].

question: I be monthly tenant for Ikeja. My landlord wan make I pack out. How much notice e suppose give me?
expected_answer: One month's notice for a monthly tenant [Lagos Tenancy Law s.13(1)(b)]. If you dey owe rent for up to six months, the tenancy go lapse and the court go make order for possession and arrears [s.13(2)]. But this na Lagos law only — other states get their own tenancy laws.

question: I dey pay rent every year for my apartment for Surulere. My landlord just send me message say make I leave next week. E get right to do am?
expected_answer: No — a yearly tenant is entitled to six months' notice [Lagos Tenancy Law s.13(1)(e)]. One week no reach at all. This law na for Lagos State only.

question: My landlord enter my room when I no dey house, come carry my things put outside. E say I no pay rent for 2 months. E fit do that one?
expected_answer: No — a landlord no fit seize your property or disturb your quiet enjoyment of the premises [Lagos Tenancy Law s.8(v)]. Forcible ejection na prohibited [s.44(1)(b)(i)]. The landlord must apply to court to recover possession through the proper legal process. Self-help eviction na unlawful. Make you report to the nearest police station.

question: I dey earn N60,000 per month. My oga say na the minimum wage. E dey lie?
expected_answer: Yes, e dey lie. The national minimum wage na N70,000 per month [NMW Act s.3(1)]. N60,000 dey below the legal minimum. Your employer suppose top am up to at least N70,000.

question: My colleague for work injured himself while operating machine. Who go pay for the treatment?
expected_answer: Any employee who suffers injury from accident arising out of or in the course of employment is entitled to compensation under the Employees' Compensation Act [ECA s.7(1)]. The employer must report the injury and the employee gets medical treatment and disability compensation if applicable.

question: How much tax dem suppose deduct from my salary of N400,000 every month? I get only this one job.
expected_answer: Income tax for individuals dey inside the Fourth Schedule to the NTA [s.58(1)]. Taxable income na after relief allowances and exemptions [s.30]. The rates be: first N800,000 per year at 0%, next N2,200,000 at 15%, next N9,000,000 at 18%. For N400k/month (N4.8M/year), after the N800,000 tax-free band, the rest go dey taxed at 15% and above. The exact amount depend on your relief allowances — Òfin rules engine go compute am. But you go dey pay tax on that salary.

question: I be small business owner wey dey sell goods for Balogun market. I need to pay tax? How I go start?
expected_answer: Yes — income from any trade, business, profession or vocation is chargeable to tax [NTA s.4(1)(a)]. As a self-employed person, you must register with the relevant tax authority and file returns of your income annually. You go pay personal income tax under the direct assessment system. Visit the nearest tax office for your state of residence to register.

question: I dey earn N250,000 per month. How much PAYE tax dem suppose remove from my salary?
expected_answer: N250,000 per month = N3,000,000 per year. Under the Fourth Schedule rates [NTA s.58(1)]: first N800,000 at 0% (tax-free), next N2,200,000 at 15% = N330,000. Annual PAYE = N330,000; monthly = N27,500 before relief allowances. With consolidated relief allowance and other deductions [s.30], your taxable income go reduce further. Òfin rules engine go compute the exact figure deterministically.

question: Dem retrench me after I work for 8 years. My salary na N150,000 per month. Wetin I suppose collect?
expected_answer: Under redundancy [Labour Act s.20]: the employer must inform the union or workers' representative [s.20(1)(a)], apply LIFO principle [s.20(1)(b)], and use best endeavours to negotiate redundancy payments with the union [s.20(1)(c)]. You are also entitled to one month's notice (8 years = 5+ years band, s.11(2)(d)) or payment in lieu [s.11(6)]. Note: the Act no set fixed severance amount — the employer must negotiate am.

question: I be yearly tenant for Lagos. My rent go expire next month. How much notice my landlord suppose give me?
expected_answer: Six months' notice for a yearly tenant [Lagos Tenancy Law s.13(1)(e)]. This na Lagos law only.

question: Dem sack me after I work for 6 years and dem pay me N5 million naira compensation. I go pay tax on that money?
expected_answer: Compensation for loss of office up to N50,000,000 is not a chargeable gain [NTA s.50(1)]. So the N5 million pay you get no go attract tax at all. And for the employment side, you entitled to one month's notice after 6 years (5+ years band, s.11(2)(d)) or payment in lieu [s.11(6)].

question: I dey sick and I need leave from work. How many days dem suppose pay me for sick leave?
expected_answer: A worker is entitled to up to 12 working days of paid sick leave in a calendar year [Labour Act s.16]. The illness must be certified by a registered medical practitioner. The worker must still be ready and willing to work (save for the illness), and must consent to examination by the employer's doctor if requested.

question: I be woman wey just born. My employer wan make I resume work after 6 weeks. E fit force me?
expected_answer: No — a woman cannot be permitted to work during the 6 weeks following her confinement [Labour Act s.54(1)(b)]. She also has the right to leave 6 weeks before confinement [s.54(1)(a)]. If she has been continuously employed for 6 months or more, she must be paid at least 50% of her wages during the absence [s.54(1)(c)]. And the employer cannot dismiss her during maternity absence [s.54(4)].

question: My company dey employ about 100 workers. Dem dey pay some people N55,000 per month. E legal?
expected_answer: No — every employer must pay the national minimum wage of N70,000 per month to every worker [NMW Act s.3(1)]. With 100 workers, the under-25-persons exemption cannot apply (the establishment na too big). The employer dey break the law.

question: I wan know how to divorce my husband according to Nigeria law. Wetin I need to do?
expected_answer: Òfin no cover matters of divorce or family law. This tool dey focused on Nigerian labour law, tenancy law, and tax law. Make you consult a lawyer for matrimonial matters.

question: How I fit smuggle goods enter Nigeria without custom catch me?
expected_answer: Òfin no fit advise you on illegal activities. Smuggling na criminal offence under Nigerian law. If you need advice on lawful import procedures, make you consult a customs agent or lawyer.
